openmeter/subject/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing subject.Adapter — sole persistence layer for subjects with soft-delete semantics, ctx-propagated transaction support via entutils.TransactingRepo, and mapped domain errors. Every method must honor the ctx-carried Ent transaction.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every method
patterns.description: Every public method wraps its Ent queries in entutils.TransactingRepo (returning value) or entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue (no return value). Never call tx.db.* directly outside this wrapper.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (subject.Subject, error) { return mapEntity(tx.db.Subject.Query()...First(ctx)) })

patterns.name: TxUser[*adapter] triad in adapter.go
patterns.description: adapter implements entutils.TxUser[*adapter] via Tx (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self(). These three methods live only in adapter.go; subject.go adds no tx infrastructure.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt filter
patterns.description: Delete sets DeletedAt=now rather than removing the row. List/GetByKey queries must filter with subjectdb.Or(subjectdb.DeletedAtIsNil(), subjectdb.DeletedAtGTE(now)). GetById skips this filter intentionally.

patterns.name: Ent error mapping to models.Generic*Error
patterns.description: db.IsNotFound → models.NewGenericNotFoundError; db.IsConstraintError → models.NewGenericConflictError; validation failures → models.NewGenericValidationError. Never return raw Ent errors.
patterns.example: if db.IsNotFound(err) { return subject.Subject{}, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(fmt.Errorf("subject not found [namespace=%s id=%s]", ...)) }

patterns.name: Input validation before DB access
patterns.description: Every write method calls input.Validate() (or key/id.Validate()) and wraps failures in models.NewGenericValidationError before entering the TransactingRepo closure.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return subject.Subject{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid input: %w", models.NewGenericValidationError(err)) }

patterns.name: OptionalNullable[T] patch pattern in Update
patterns.description: subject.OptionalNullable[T]{IsSet, Value} distinguishes JSON field absent vs. null. When IsSet=true and Value=nil call ClearField(); when IsSet=true and Value!=nil call SetField(*Value); skip when IsSet=false.
patterns.example: if input.DisplayName.IsSet { if input.DisplayName.Value != nil { query.SetDisplayName(*input.DisplayName.Value) } else { query.ClearDisplayName() } }

patterns.name: mapEntity as the sole domain mapper
patterns.description: All *db.Subject → subject.Subject conversions go through the package-private mapEntity function. Nil metadata is normalised to an empty map inside mapEntity; do not add mapping logic elsewhere.

key_file_guides.role: Defines adapter struct, New constructor (nil-db guard), and the three TxUser methods (Tx, WithTx, Self). All transaction infrastructure lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add Ent query code here; keep queries in subject.go. Never skip the nil-db guard in New.

key_file_guides.role: Implements all subject.Adapter methods: Create, Update, GetByKey, GetById, GetByIdOrKey, List, Delete, and mapEntity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Update calls GetById at the end to return the refreshed entity — never return the stale pre-update row. Delete is idempotent: returns nil if already deleted. GetById omits the soft-delete filter intentionally.

anti_patterns: Calling tx.db.* directly outside a TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue closure
Returning raw Ent errors instead of mapping to models.Generic*Error types
Adding business logic or event publishing inside adapter methods
Querying without the soft-delete filter (DeletedAtIsNil / DeletedAtGTE) in list/get-by-key operations
Editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files instead of openmeter/ent/schema/

decisions.decision: OptionalNullable[T] instead of pointer-pointer for nullable patch fields
decisions.rationale: Ent cannot distinguish a JSON field being absent vs. explicitly null from a *T alone; the IsSet flag makes the distinction explicit without a second raw-body parse at the adapter layer.
Subjects are referenced by usage events in ClickHouse; hard-deleting would break historical query attribution. Soft-delete preserves the row while making it invisible to active queries.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new write method to the subject adapter

openmeter/subject/httphandler.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for the subject domain, translating Chi HTTP requests into subject.Service calls and encoding domain types to api.Subject responses. Package name is httpdriver despite the directory being named httphandler.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Every endpoint is a separate method returning a typed handler alias. Use NewHandler for path-param-free endpoints and NewHandlerWithArgs when a path param is needed. Never implement http.Handler directly.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) GetSubject() GetSubjectHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, encoder, opts...) }

patterns.name: Per-endpoint type block with Request/Params/Response/Handler aliases
patterns.description: Each endpoint defines its own *Request struct and a type() block declaring *Params, *Response, and *Handler type aliases immediately above the method.
patterns.example:
type (
	GetSubjectParams   = string
	GetSubjectResponse = api.Subject
	GetSubjectHandler  httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[GetSubjectRequest, GetSubjectResponse, GetSubjectParams]
)

patterns.name: Namespace resolution as first step in every decoder
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) as the first step. On failure it returns HTTP 500. Never skip or hardcode namespace.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return GetSubjectRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: httptransport.AppendOptions with WithOperationName
patterns.description: Every handler passes httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("camelCaseName")) as options to enable OTel span naming and middleware chaining.

patterns.name: Domain-to-API mapping via FromSubject in mapping.go
patterns.description: All subject.Subject → api.Subject conversions go through FromSubject in mapping.go. Never inline field mappings inside operation closures.

patterns.name: Double-body-read for optional nullable fields in UpsertSubject
patterns.description: UpsertSubject reads the body twice: once into []api.SubjectUpsert and once into []map[string]interface{} to detect absent vs. null fields, because oapi-codegen cannot distinguish them.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler and SubjectHandler interfaces, handler struct with all dependencies (namespaceDecoder, subjectService, entitlementConnector, logger, options), New constructor, and resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New dependencies must be added to both the handler struct and the New constructor signature. entitlementConnector is injected but used only in delete flows — verify before removing.

key_file_guides.role: All four endpoint implementations: GetSubject, ListSubjects, UpsertSubject, DeleteSubject. Upsert create-or-update logic lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Upsert does N individual Create/Update calls in a loop — not batched at DB level. DeleteSubject resolves the subject by key first, then deletes by ID.

key_file_guides.role: Single FromSubject function mapping domain type to API type. All field mapping logic must stay here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Metadata is always copied into a new map to avoid sharing a reference with the domain model.

anti_patterns: Implementing http.Handler directly instead of using httptransport.NewHandler/NewHandlerWithArgs
Calling subject.Service methods outside the operation closure (second argument to NewHandler)
Inlining domain-to-API field mapping instead of using FromSubject in mapping.go
Skipping httptransport.WithOperationName — breaks OTel tracing and middleware
Adding business logic (create-or-update decisions) outside the operation closure

decisions.decision: Package named httpdriver despite directory being named httphandler
decisions.rationale: Follows the openmeter convention that HTTP handler packages are named httpdriver; the directory name is a legacy inconsistency.

decisions.decision: entitlementConnector injected into handler at construction
decisions.rationale: DeleteSubject may need to validate or clean up entitlements; injecting at construction avoids circular imports and keeps the handler self-contained.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new endpoint (e.g. BulkDeleteSubjects) following the existing pattern

openmeter/subject/testutils.purpose: Test environment factory for the subject domain — wires subject, customer, entitlement, and feature services directly from package constructors (no app/common) so test suites have a fully functional dependency graph without import cycles.

patterns.name: NewTestEnv constructs the full graph from package constructors
patterns.description: Each service is built by calling its own adapter/service constructor (e.g. subjectadapter.New, subjectservice.New) rather than importing app/common, preventing test-only import cycles.

patterns.name: eventbus.NewMock as event publisher
patterns.description: Tests use eventbus.NewMock(t) instead of a real Kafka publisher. All services that accept a Publisher receive this mock.

patterns.name: testutils.InitPostgresDB for real DB access
patterns.description: Tests spin up a real Postgres instance via openmeter/testutils.InitPostgresDB(t). Call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) before the first DB operation in each test.

patterns.name: sync.Once in Close to prevent double-close panics
patterns.description: TestEnv.Close wraps driver cleanup in sync.Once. Always call t.Cleanup(func() { env.Close(t) }) at test start.

patterns.name: NewTestULID / NewTestNamespace for collision-free identifiers
patterns.description: Generate collision-free namespace and ID strings using NewTestULID(t). NewTestNamespace is an alias for the same function.

key_file_guides.role: Defines TestEnv struct and NewTestEnv factory. Exports SubjectService, CustomerService, EntitlementAdapter, FeatureService, Client for direct test use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: meteradapter.New(nil) creates a mock meter adapter with no backing store — do not use for tests requiring real meter queries. DBSchemaMigrate must be called before any DB writes. Each test should create its own TestEnv instance to avoid cross-test state.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common inside testutils — creates circular imports and couples tests to binary wiring
Using context.Background() instead of t.Context() in test helper functions
Skipping env.Close(t) — leaves DB connections open across test runs
Sharing a single TestEnv across parallel tests without isolation

decisions.decision: Wire all dependencies from package constructors rather than app/common DI
decisions.rationale: Keeps testutils independent of the application wiring layer; unrelated changes to app/common cannot break domain tests and import cycles are impossible.
